description: Documentation and Ralph guidance for pibo debug pty usage, artifacts, host vs Docker execution, real-provider safety, and evidence requirements.

userStories.title: Document pibo debug pty usage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want concise pibo debug pty examples so that I can run PTY checks without reading implementation code.
acceptanceCriteria: Add docs or help text for pibo debug pty run, scenario, and list-scenarios
Include a host execution example and a Docker-worker execution example
Include examples for rows, cols, timeout, idle timeout, input delay, expect, reject, and artifact dir
Docs match the implemented command behavior
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document artifacts and debugging workflow
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want artifact documentation so that I can inspect PTY failures and understand what the user saw.
acceptanceCriteria: Document metadata.json, input.json, assertions.json, raw.ansi.log, clean.txt, and screen.txt
Explain when to inspect raw ANSI versus cleaned text
Explain how failure output points to the artifact directory
Warn that artifacts may contain transcripts, provider output, and secrets
Docs include one sample failure interpretation
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document provider safety rules
userStories.description: As an operator, I want real-provider safety rules documented so that live PTY smokes are explicit and bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Document mocked/deterministic provider mode as the default
Document that live provider calls require --real-provider
Document default --max-iterations 10 for real-provider mode
Document wall-clock timeout, idle timeout, stop conditions, and fail-closed behavior
Document that real-provider PTY E2E is not part of default npm test
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Update Ralph guidance for CLI and TUI validation
userStories.description: As a Ralph implementation agent, I want clear validation gates so that interactive terminal stories are not marked complete after weak fake/demo checks only.
acceptanceCriteria: Update Ralph or PRD guidance to require PTY scenario validation when interactive CLI/TUI behavior changes
Guidance requires evidence notes with commands run, host/Docker target, provider mode, artifact path, and fake/demo/mocked/real classification
Guidance states that fake/demo checks are supporting evidence and cannot replace real default-path checks when the user path is in scope
Guidance references the canonical CLI Session UI scenario where appropriate
Focused docs/skill validation checks pass if available
Typecheck passes
